pattern: Since one popcount step shrinks any value below 1e15 to at most 50, depth is determined by the depth of the bit-count, so the task becomes counting numbers up to n with a fixed number of set bits via digit combinatorics.
hints: For x > 1 the depth is one more than the depth of popcount(x), and popcount(x) is tiny, so precompute the depth of every small integer up to about sixty.
Counting integers in [1, n] whose depth is k reduces to counting those whose set-bit count is one of the small values having depth k-1.
Count integers up to n with exactly c set bits by scanning n's bits high to low and using binomial choices for the free lower bits; remember x = 1 has depth 0, not 1.

edge_inputs.description: depth one excludes x=1 despite its single bit
k larger than any reachable depth in range

stress.description: random moderate n with each valid k, checked against a brute per-integer depth oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    k = rng.randrange(0, 6)
    return [n, k]
